steps.title: Identify what the narrative opening must do
steps.content: The task asks for a narrative that begins with a specific athlete's story, using career details as a launching point. The opening must focus on one athlete, anchor itself in the start of that career, and use a concrete team reference from the notes (e.g., the New England Patriots).

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the match
steps.content:
• "Carter began his career with the New England Patriots; Hill started with the Cleveland Browns.": Immediately splits focus between both athletes rather than opening with one. ✗
  • "When Carter put on his New England Patriots uniform for his first NFL game, he began what would become a legendary professional career.": Opens on Carter alone, anchors the narrative at the very start of his career with a concrete team detail, and signals future achievement. ✓
  • "While Carter finished his career with more rushing yards, both Carter and Hill reached the career milestone of rushing for over 10,000 yards total.": Compares both athletes and focuses on career outcomes, not the career's outset. ✗
  • "Carter's blend of speed, power, and agility helped him accrue 14,101 rushing yards by the end of his career, etching his name into the NFL's record books.": Focuses on the end of Carter's career rather than the beginning. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
